entry.summary: Refactoring the dev panel into divergent section-vs-band surfaces regressed the operator workflow: the compact panel kept good ids, while the long section panel drifted away from the sticky compact UX and source-searchable copy tokens.
entry.context: During public-site dev-id work on 2026-04-14, the shared panel logic was split into a compact band panel (used by nav/footer and later page bands) and a longer section scan panel. The compact panel was closer to the desired operator workflow, but the section panel kept a different layout and copied less useful tokens in some paths. At the same time, some section shells were still relying on auto-generated runtime ids, which made copied values hard to grep in source.
entry.failure: Two mistakes combined: (1) treated "section panel UX" and "band panel UX" as separate products instead of one operator surface with different scopes; (2) let runtime-generated ids remain in the path where the operator actually copies identifiers for Codex/Cursor/Claude handoff. The result was inconsistency exactly where the panel needs to be most deterministic.
entry.rule: The dev panel is one product. Do not maintain separate compact-vs-long semantics for the same actions. Any panel row or prompt handoff must prefer a source-searchable literal (`cid`, `stableCid`, `stableBandCid`, or `as`) over a runtime-only generated id. If a refactor improves one panel mode but not the other, stop and unify the surface before extending it further.

entry.summary: Hand-converted markdown planning files to XML instead of using a CLI command — the CLI command does not exist, but the gap should have been surfaced before doing the conversion.
entry.context: During app-forge project scaffolding on 2026-04-14, ran `gad projects init --name app-forge --path apps/forge`. The CLI emitted four markdown template files (PROJECT.md, REQUIREMENTS.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md) containing literal placeholder content from the new-project skill template. The rest of the monorepo uses 6+ canonical XML files (STATE.xml, ROADMAP.xml, TASK-REGISTRY.xml, DECISIONS.xml, ERRORS-AND-ATTEMPTS.xml, REQUIREMENTS.xml). After being asked to convert to XML, I hand-wrote the XML files instead of using a CLI command.
entry.failure: Two compounding errors: (1) Used `gad projects init` without first checking whether its output matched the repo's canonical format. The misalignment between "what init scaffolds" and "what every other planning root looks like" should have been a stop-and-flag moment before any files were written. (2) When asked to convert, hand-rolled the XML instead of looking for or building a `gad migrate-schema --from md --to xml` (or equivalent) command. `gad migrate-schema --help` only shows RP→GAD-Markdown conversion. The right move was to surface the gap as a framework task in this file and the task registry, then ask the user whether to add the CLI command first or hand-convert as a one-off — not silently hand-roll.
entry.rule:
Before doing any structural change to planning files (format conversion, schema migration, file-set normalization), look for a `gad <verb>` command that does it. If none exists, STOP and surface the gap as a task against `get-anything-done` and an entry in this file. Then ask the user: "no CLI command for this — should I (a) add one to GAD first, or (b) hand-convert as a one-off?" Never silently hand-roll structural changes.
Also: after running `gad projects init` for a new planning root, immediately verify the output matches the canonical file set used elsewhere in the repo. Drift between "what init scaffolds" and "what reality looks like" is a framework gap that must be filed against `get-anything-done`, not papered over.

entry.summary: Mermaid syntax errors were rendered as leaked body text below page content, visually pushing footer context and confusing landmark-driven editing on /gad.
entry.context: During speech-driven UPDATE testing against `/gad`, the operator reported text like "Mermaid JS syntax error ... mermaid version 11.14.0" appearing below the page and interfering with footer-area reasoning. The target landmark was `site-footer`, but root cause was not Footer itself.
entry.failure: Mermaid render failures in workflow diagram components could leave temporary error nodes in `document.body`, outside component hosts. Those leaked nodes persisted across navigation and looked like footer/layout regressions on unrelated routes.
entry.rule: For visual-landmark debugging, resolve by landmark first but always validate cross-route side effects from shared client renderers. Mermaid/graph renderers must clean up temporary DOM artifacts after each render attempt, and leaked error nodes must be removed unless inside explicit diagram hosts.

entry.summary: gad-tools-models test: 'honors explicit model overrides' fails pre-existing — resolveAgentModel returns alias not full ID
entry.context: Test at line 64 passes model_overrides={gad-planner: 'claude-sonnet-4-6'} and expects planner_model to be 'claude-sonnet-4-6', but resolveAgentModel returns 'sonnet' alias instead of full model ID string.
entry.failure: AssertionError: actual 'sonnet' expected 'claude-sonnet-4-6'. Pre-existed before phase 73 work.
entry.rule: resolveAgentModel returns short aliases (sonnet, haiku, inherit) not full model IDs. Tests expecting full IDs must fix normalizeOverride to pass through unknown strings, or match alias behavior.

entry.summary: tests/commands.test.cjs: 75 pre-existing failures — "Unknown command: stats" and "Unknown command: check-commit" in gad-tools calls
entry.context: Verified before and after phase-74 changes by running node --test tests/commands.test.cjs. Both runs report exactly 75 failures, 0 passes. Failures involve Unknown command: stats and Unknown command: check-commit/progress subcommand json. Unrelated to friction-fix scope.
entry.failure: AssertionError: Command failed: Error: Unknown command: stats / check-commit. Pre-existed before phase 74.
entry.rule: tests/commands.test.cjs requires gad-tools commands (stats, check-commit) that are not yet implemented. Do not treat as regression from CLI argv or decisions changes. Track against phase 73 (gad-tools verb gaps).
